title: COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM - 211info
text: NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management, individual and family support plans, access to brokerage services for transportation, in-home supports or residential placement, and employment and vocational services. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 523-8366 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 523-8366 Administration (541) 519-3940 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to request an application or schedule an intake appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of citizenship, proof of address, and records of any/all psychological and medical assessments related to the disability. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Baker County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Exit 304 off of I-84 Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Disabilities Issues Case/Care Management * Disabilities Issues Disability Related Center Based Employment Disability Related Parenting Programs Disability Related Transportation Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Residential Placement Services for People with Disabilities Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH AND SUD ADDICTIONS EMPLOYMENT - SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
